review_text: People with sensitive skin beware it has niaminicide!! I have rosacea and after reading that PHA could be a good option as an exfoliant for sensitive skin I ordered this product. I didn’t realize it had niaminicide also. After using it my entire face turned red and had a slight burning. Putting acids and niaminicide together causes irritation and can even cause the products to not work as well. I don’t understand why the company would take a product recommended for sensitive skin and put something in it that makes it terrible for sensitive skin.

review_text: I have been using the St Tropez mousse now for a couple months. I have light skin golden undertones. The color it gives my skin is a nice olive glow that looks good if I don’t layer on too much. The smell is tolerable. I exfoliate before applying. It is very drying. I use a pump of lotion on the mitt and mix it with a pump of the tanning mousse it helps keep my skin hydrated. It does go on nicely doesn’t streak. I pay attention to making sure it is blended in nicely. It is sticky after applied. It takes several hours for the tan to develop. It lasts maybe 3 days if you don’t exfoliate or shave too much. Need to moisturize twice a day to stop skin from getting scales.  It does rub off onto my clothes and sheets which is bad because I like to wear white in the summer time. It doesn’t stain though.

It has such a light scent so if you don’t like heavily scented skincare products then this is perfect for you! review_text: I was super excited when I discovered I was receiving a free sample of this product from Influenster. I suffer from acne prone skin. As I was getting my break outs under control I really needed a product to help with scarring. If you are pressed for time this is a terrible product for time management.

review_text: upper lip and chin area. And it can get very distinct and there are weird cut-off areas where my skin will be spared from the redness. It’s definitely due to how my capillaries are formed; my skin type & my inflammation. Even THAT calms down noticeably after using this; and I didn’t have to sit in a mirror and think hard about it to deduce that. It also will help calm the anger in any active breakouts & reduce the redness in healing inflamed acne scars. I have been using this along with some other great skin products so I can’t contribute my lack of active surface acne to just this- but it’s definitely played a huge part in adding a penetrating bounce to my skin and giving it even & ethereal “milky“ complexion. As someone who’s oil production naturally alternates from being on slight overdrive to super overdrive; I feel like this alone encourages my oil glands to back down a little bit as well. I LOVE how you don’t have to rinse it off and I love how the impact of the anti-inflammatory effects don’t just last an hour or 2 like some other products with a similar aim. If you give it enough time between waking up & makeup application; it won’t pill under your makeup- at least it hasn’t for me and I like to play with multiple different formulations of primers etc. It’s just thick enough to give dry skin instant relief yet it’s very emollient so a little goes a long way; even on skin I’ve just toned with a generous application of a powerful Glycolic & Lactic Citrus toner. To add perspective; I’ll include a photo where I’ve gone 2 nights with poor sleep right after using it.

review_text: This product was gifted by Murad. I have blemish prone skin and a couple times a month tend to get those deep under the surface type bumps that just take forever to go away. I was so excited to see this treatment and couldn’t wait to try it out. This contains 2% salicylic acid and I think that may not have been strong enough to work for me. Typically that strength is what I use on spots that are already to a head or have already been popped and I need to reduce the redness and inflammation fast. I’ve found that for me a 10% benzoyl peroxide works better for subsurface bumps. This one didn’t help to reduce any of my deep spots but did help to alleviate a couple minor bumps that were already “up“. If you have similar skin issues to me you might want to skip this one.

review_text: I received this product FREE for testing purposes. I’m giving it three stars because I wasn’t super impressed. I decided the best way to really test this product was to ditch my tried and true Clinique DDMG and switch to this. I’m almost at the bottom of the bottle and feel I have used it long enough to form an opinion. I won’t be purchasing this. For $68 and only 30ml I think that’s EXPENSIVE. I was willing to give it a try and see if it was worth the claims. Yet after using the whole bottle I honestly saw no difference in my skin. I have combination oily skin and didn’t see any difference at all. I did like that it felt light weight and not greasy. That being said the first time I put it on I was a little taken back at the immediate tacky feeling it gave me. It fades quickly but it felt weird for a second. Similar to a jelly moisturizer which is not my journey. Overall I didn’t think it was a bad product but I personally wouldn’t purchase it. I suggest getting a sample (if we can ever do that again) and trying it for at least a couple days before making the purchase.

I got some of this in a sample and tried it for a few days and I’m seriously so surprised that it works this well. I bought and returned the cream because it was too thick and couldn’t get it to work for me. I haven’t even been needing to use foundation over this though! I’ll be buying a full size as soon as I can get to Sephora.

review_text: that being said don’t use this sunscreen as your SPF replacement. It gets diluted when its put into the moisturizer. This moisturizer has a nice texture to it. It has a pinky tone to the cream but when you apply it there is no color seen It does not leave a white cast on your skin. It’s not shiny but gives more of a dewy look.The packaging is fantastic it’s a refillable cartridge inside the bottle so you don’t waste the bottle. Rihanna said that she wanted her products to be bought once and then be used over and over again. Less pollution to our environment which I hope continues across other brands as well. Overall ingredients decent nothing revolutionary. SPF is wonderful to see in the first 3 products. A decent chemical sunscreen inside the moisturizer and great packaging.  Retails for $35 a mid-price point.

review_text: I don’t like to spend a lot of money on things like this & I try to stay away from skincare especially on my face with added fragrance or anything that might develop allergies towards but other than that I really enjoy this. I’m very fair Irish complected even though I live in Florida on the beach. I can’t wear makeup to jiu jitsu training and my face never matches my body(wear your sunscreen;) especially when I’m self tanned on my body. I have cystic acne prone skin so I’m scared to put anything new on my face but I thought I’d give this a try and I love it. I used two drops in my moisturizer over my acne medication(azelaic acid/clindamycin/niacinamide) which I thought surely would disrupt up but it didn’t.  I also used the Mario badescu pink spot treatment on top one night and didn’t have spots. It didn’t cling to my existing acne or make dark spots obvious like my body tanner has in the past!! I use toner in the morning and figured that would strip it off but the toner pad was pretty clean maybe just a tad on the surface came off. I took this down to my neck and could tell I didn’t blend it well enough hence the picture. Buildable color-that was one day use. My eyebrows didn’t stain but I dye my eyebrows brown and I didn’t try to put tons of moisturizer to get stuck in them. One thing that could make you look dirty is not blending into your hairline. I’m blonde so maybe it’s more obvious but the first night I noticed where the color stopped so I blended better and fixed it on night two. It’s maybe a little warm toned orange but i have light skin pink undertones and most tanners pull orange based to me. It’s expensive but if you can afford it it lasts a long time.
